title: Handling Dynamic Page States
concept: Waiting for elements, spinners, network idle, and SPA transitions before the agent takes its next action.

exercises.prompt: What is one action you can tell an AI agent to perform in a web browser?
exercises.explain: An AI agent can be instructed to perform actions like clicking a button to interact with web content.

cards.title: Dynamic Page States
cards.body: Web pages can change a lot while you're using them! This is called dynamic behavior and it can affect how your AI agent acts.

cards.title: What Are Dynamic Page States?
cards.body: Dynamic page states refer to changes in a web page that happen without reloading the page. This can be due to loading new data or switching views.

cards.title: Think of a Movie Loading
cards.body: Imagine watching a movie that frequently loads new scenes. You wouldn't want to start talking until the scene is fully loaded, right? That's how an AI agent waits for changes to happen!

cards.title: Waiting for Elements
cards.body: If an agent needs to click a button that appears after loading, it must wait until that button is visible.

cards.title: Myth: Agents Can Act Instantly
cards.body: Some might think that AI agents are instant. However, they need to wait for the right moment to act, especially on dynamic pages.

cards.title: Waiting Across Different States
cards.body: Let's see how waiting works in different situations:

exercises.prompt: What should an AI agent do if it sees a loading spinner on a page?
exercises.explain: The correct answer is to wait until the loading spinner disappears, ensuring the page is fully ready for interaction.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An AI agent should act immediately after clicking a button, even if the page is still loading.
exercises.explain: It's false. The agent should wait for any loading to finish to ensure the action is valid.

exercises.prompt: An AI agent needs to wait for a ______ before it can safely act on a dynamic page.
exercises.sentence: An AI agent needs to wait for a spinner before it can safely act on a dynamic page.
exercises.explain: The agent should wait for a spinner to disappear to ensure that the page is ready for interaction.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is a sign that a page is still loading?
exercises.explain: A loading spinner indicates that the page is still loading, warning the agent to wait.

exercises.prompt: When should an AI agent consider a page ready for action?

options.text: As soon as it clicks something
After any loading indicators are gone
When the screen is completely white

exercises.explain: The correct answer is that the agent should act after any loading indicators are gone, as this means the page is fully ready for interaction.
